channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 9 startups from Palantir alumni you need to know
item.description: Last week, I published a deep exploration into Palantir and its founder factory and how the company’s power and success can be explained by its ability to attract elite talent and how it empowers them to develop their skills and learn new ones in the projects they pursue.
item.dc:creator: David Lidsky, Fast Company

item.title: Chrome gets its biggest upgrade in years
item.description: Google’s biggest Chrome update in years bakes Gemini AI agents, multitasking, and image editing directly into the browser, turning everyday web browsing into a hands-off experience.
item.dc:creator: Graham Barlow, TechRadar

item.title: How K-12 schools are left on their own to develop AI policies
item.description: Generative artificial intelligence technology is rapidly reshaping education in unprecedented ways. With its potential benefits and risks, K-12 schools are actively trying to adapt teaching and learning.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon plans to cut 16,000 jobs as AI competition heats up
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. announced plans to terminate about 16,000 corporate employees, ratcheting up efforts to streamline bureaucracy amid rising competition over artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: LinkedIn will now let you show off how good you are with AI tools
item.description: LinkedIn doesn't just want everyone to have a verification badge, it's also offering AI proficiency badges with select partners.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Silent Hill fans have a great excuse to check out Fatal Frame 2: Crimson Butterfly Remake as Koei Tecmo and Konami announce collaboration
item.description: Koei Tecmo announces Fatal Frame 2: Crimson Butterfly Remake demo and a collaboration with one of the best horror games of the decade.
item.dc:creator: Rhys Wood, TechRadar

item.title: Newsom zeroes out California’s landmark deal to save local news
item.description: Local news has suffered a long decline since the advent of the internet shifted the ads newspapers and other outlets relied upon into the digital realm.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Meta-Corning fiber deal signals a new bottleneck in AI infrastructure
item.description: Meta has announced a $6 billion, multi-year fiber supply agreement with Corning, highlighting how AI infrastructure constraints are shifting beyond compute and into the physical network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: To fix federal HR, Washington must stop rewarding failure
item.description: This is the "maintenance trap" in action: Agencies spend so much time and money keeping broken systems running that they can't afford to replace them. The post To fix federal HR, Washington must stop rewarding failure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How to watch A Super Progressive Movie online from anywhere
item.description: Here’s how you can watch A Super Progressive Movie online and from anywhere as the Australian political satire hits streaming and stokes debate around the culture wars.
item.dc:creator: Bill Borrows, TechRadar

item.title: Disruptive innovation is key to building world-changing companies, but it needs a moral compass in the age of AI
item.description: At the Consumer Electronics Show in early January, Razer made waves by unveiling a small jar containing a holographic anime bot designed to accompany gamers not just during gameplay, but in daily life.
item.dc:creator: Julia Freeland Fisher, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic cofounder Daniela Amodei says trusted enterprise AI will transcend the hype cycle
item.description: While Silicon Valley argues over bubbles, benchmarks, and who has the smartest model, Anthropic has been focused on solving problems that rarely generate hype but ultimately determine adoption: whether AI can be trusted to operate inside the world’s most sensitive systems.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: It’s time to upgrade those old Intel Macs
item.description: IT admins and CIOs should be aware that 2026 is effectively the final year of support for Intel-based Macs. Apple told us so at WWDC last year when it said, “macOS 26 (Tahoe) will be the final release for Intel Macs.” That means macOS 27 won’t support Intel chips.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Restrictive H‑1B policies drive tech talent back to India, reshaping global IT
item.description: US enterprises are experiencing a fundamental reversal in global technology talent flows as restrictive H-1B visa policies and India’s rapidly maturing tech ecosystem combine to redirect skilled professionals away from US careers, threatening American competitiveness while accelerating India’s emerg
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: OpenAI’s GPT is getting better at mathematics
item.description: OpenAI’s GPT-5.2 Pro does better at solving sophisticated math problems than older versions of the company’s top large language model, according to a new study by Epoch AI, a non-profit research institute.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: AMD news and insights | Nvidia news and insights Intel is hoping for a turnaround under its new CEO, Lip-BuTan. Intel’s Q1 2025 revenue was $12.7 billion, flat year-over-year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Winter storms can knock out your tech fast: Prepare now
item.description: Major winter storm threatens widespread power outages across the US starting Friday. Essential tech prep tips to keep devices charged and stay connected.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GitHub Copilot SDK allows developers to build Copilot agents into apps
item.description: GitHub has launched a technical preview of the GitHub Copilot SDK, a tool kit for embedding the “agentic core” of the GitHub Copilot CLI into applications. Available on GitHub, the SDK was unveiled on January 22. Initially available for Node.js/TypeScript, Python, Go, and .
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FedRAMP is getting faster, new automation and pilots promise approvals in months, not years
item.description: "If you want outcomes, you need to get these technologies into the hands of our agencies today," said Irina Denisenko. The post FedRAMP is getting faster, new automation and pilots promise approvals in months, not years first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Reports of SATA’s demise are overblown, but the technology is aging fast
item.description: With the upheaval taking place in the memory market, a new rumor has emerged about the demise of the Serial Advanced Technology Attachment (SATA) drive interface.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Workforce, supply chain factor into reauthorizing National Quantum Initiative
item.description: House and Senate lawmakers are both advancing legislation to reauthorize and update the National Quantum Initiative. The post Workforce, supply chain factor into reauthorizing National Quantum Initiative first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel nabs Qualcomm veteran to lead GPU initiative
item.description: Intel and Qualcomm have traded executives several times over the years, and the trading continues with Intel luring away Qualcomm’s GPU lead. Eric Demers, a long-time industry veteran GPU engineer, joins Intel as senior vice president and the lead of its GPU engineering team.
item.dc:creator: Network World
